title: Rollandia pinnatifida
taxonomy: Rollandia pinnatifida is a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae, commonly known as the nightshade family. This family includes a wide range of plants, from edible species like tomatoes and potatoes to toxic ones like deadly nightshade. The Solanaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Rollandia pinnatifida likely belonging to the subfamily Solanoideae. The plant is native to South America, specifically in the mountainous regions of the Andes. More research is needed to confirm the specific classification of Rollandia pinnatifida within the Solanaceae family.

intro: Rollandia pinnatifida, also known as Yerba de Mayo or Mayo Herb, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help in managing conditions like digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems.
